title: Malware Removal, Media Sanitization, and Incident Evidence · Stakeholder Support Mission
summary: An incident coordinator, affected worker, identity owner, data custodian, privacy reviewer, and media disposition authority. They need proportionate isolation, identity and endpoint evidence, trusted recovery, sanitization selection and validation, chain of custody, accessible communication, and no unsupported forensic or legal claim. Planned artifact: Deliver Endpoint Malware, Account, and Media Incident Response with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint ex…
objectives: Protect people, preserve evidence, confirm authority, isolate proportionally, avoid tipping or destroying evidence, use trusted communication, and escalate under the response plan.
Apply least privilege, need to know, default deny, separation of duties, just-in-time access, elevation, review, revocation, and emergency access.
Test original user tasks, assistive technology, remote and offline access, updates, recovery, logs, exceptions, changed identities, and hostile cases after hardening.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Project First Response
steps.instruction:
project case — A compromised encrypted endpoint needs recovery and reuse while credential exposure, persistence, legal hold, custody, and media method remain unknown. The project variation 4 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: defend one safe causal support decision

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Project First Response.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for protect people, preserve evidence, confirm authority, isolate proportionally, avoid tipping or destroying evidence, use trusted communication, and escalate under the response plan. For CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Project First Response, keep First Response connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A compromised encrypted endpoint needs recovery and reuse while credential exposure, persistence, legal hold, custody, and media method remain unknown. The project variation 4 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: defend one safe causal support decision

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which First Response condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: NIST Guidelines for Media Sanitization — Controls risk-based clear, purge, destroy, verification, validation, and documentation decisions for media disposition.

hints: For CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Project First Response, name the user impact, fictional ticket, asset, environment, authorization, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, accessible documentation, and transfer boundary, First Response precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Project First Response, trace this concrete input and predict user impact, safety and authority, baseline, reproduction, timeline, hypothesis, bounded test, observation, rejection, repair, rollback, original and changed-case verification, prevention, escalation, and controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A compromised encrypted endpoint needs recovery and reuse while credential exposure, persistence, legal hold, custody, and media method remain unknown. The project variation 4 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: defend one safe causal support decision

For CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Project First Response, challenge the documented misconception with changed-user, device, version, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, access need, hypothesis, test result, repair, restart, recurrence, escalation, and controlled equipment-transfer evidence: The first responder should immediately delete suspicious files to limit damage.

options.text: For commit to the mission model, apply one familiar repair, omit user impact, authorization, safety, asset and version identity, baseline, recent change, competing cause, prediction, observation, rollback, changed-case verification, accessibility, privacy, escalation, and controlled transfer, then accept a missing symptom as support proof. This does not satisfy First Response for contained recovery and media-disposition evidence packet.
State the First Response preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.
